description: Microsoft Teams app / bot framework credentials, incoming webhook URLs, outgoing webhook HMAC, and tenant red flags. Largely overlaps with Entra ID for the OAuth side — see auth-security for that.

token_patterns.name: Azure AD / Entra Client Secret (Teams app)

misconfigurations.title: Teams Incoming Webhook URL committed to repo
misconfigurations.rationale: Bearer-equivalent: leaks lead to channel spam / phishing-from-inside-the-tenant.
misconfigurations.fix: Move to Workflows / Power Automate flow with auth, or use a Bot Framework app that requires a signed token; revoke the leaked webhook from the channel.

misconfigurations.title: Outgoing webhook handler does not verify HMAC against the configured secret
misconfigurations.rationale: Without HMAC verification, anyone can POST commands to your bot endpoint.
misconfigurations.fix: Compute HMAC-SHA256 over the request body with the secret shown when the outgoing webhook was created; compare to `Authorization: HMAC <base64>` header.

misconfigurations.title: External access set to 'Allow all external domains' + anonymous meeting join
misconfigurations.rationale: Lets attackers send DMs to internal users from spoofed external tenants.
misconfigurations.fix: Teams Admin Center > Users > External access — restrict to specific allowed domains.

misconfigurations.title: Global app permission policy 'Allow all apps' for users
misconfigurations.rationale: Lets users install third-party apps which can read meeting / chat content via Graph.
misconfigurations.fix: Teams Admin Center > Teams apps > Permission policies — restrict to vetted apps.

admin_red_flags.title: New Teams app installed with a display name impersonating an internal product
admin_red_flags.rationale: Adversary-in-the-middle / phishing pattern via custom app side-loading.
admin_red_flags.signal: Audit log: `App installed` where display_name matches one of a watch-list of brand strings outside the IT-managed app catalogue.

admin_red_flags.title: Mass channel creation across multiple teams in a short window
admin_red_flags.signal: Audit log: `Channel created` count per app > 20 in 1h.

admin_red_flags.title: Bulk download of Teams meeting recordings from OneDrive/SharePoint
admin_red_flags.signal: Microsoft 365 audit: FileDownloaded events with file extension .mp4 in the user's Recordings folder, count > 10 in 1h.
